title: Finding the Subarray with Minimum Sum of Size K

content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of integers and you need to find a subarray of size KKK that has the minimum sum in all the subarray of size KKK.


Input Format

The first line contains two integers NNN and KKK, representing the size of the array and the size of the subarray, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the elements of the array.


Output Format
Output a single integer representing the minimum sum of a subarray of size KKK.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and K (1 ≤ K ≤ N). The second line contains N space‑separated integers A[i] (|A[i]| ≤ 10⁹).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer – the minimum possible sum of any contiguous subarray of size K.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first three elements (1,2,3) give the smallest sum 6.
Subarray [1,2] (positions 4 and 5) has sum 3, the minimum.
The first four elements (1,2,3,4) sum to 10, the smallest possible.

solution.approach: The sum of a subarray of length K can be updated in O(1) when we slide the window by one position: subtract the element leaving the window and add the new element entering it. Initialise the sum of the first K elements, store it as the current best, then iterate from index K to N‑1 updating the window sum and keeping the minimum. This linear scan (O(N)) per test case is optimal because every element must be examined at least once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(ΣN) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the array (can be O(1) if streamed, but O(N) is acceptable within limits).

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int T=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();while(T-- >0){st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int N=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int K=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());long[] a=new long[N];st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());for(int i=0;i<N;i++) a[i]=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());long cur=0;for(int i=0;i<K;i++) cur+=a[i];long best=cur;for(int i=K;i<N;i++){cur+=a[i]-a[i-K];if(cur<best) best=cur;}sb.append(best).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}

def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i: test case index (0 <= i <= 20)
    # RINT(a,b): random integer in [a,b]
    # POWER(a,b): a**b
    if i == 0:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('1 1')
        print('0')
    elif i == 1:
        t = 1
        print(t)
        print('1 1')
        print('-1000000000')
